title: If Someone Asked You to Explain NGE
display_title: If Someone Asked You to Explain NGE
wikitext:
[]: If Someone Asked You to "Explain" NGE...             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jun 18, 2007 6:44 pm How would you approach it? Several people have asked (or challenged) me to "explain" NGE to them. Now, after having my cousin watch it, I finally decided to sit down and write as semi-comprehensive a guide on NGE as I can. A daunting task, I know. But I would be able to "explain" things much better in print - where I'm able to organize my thoughts - than through talk. One thing that many people don't get is how MUCH of NGE there is to "explain". If you watch it through once and found yourself lost, to have someone "explain" it to you would require an incredible amount of information. Nonetheless, I'm foolishly attempting to do just that. However, how would you approach it, and what would you say/do?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Jun 18, 2007 6:59 pm I will say here what I said in the thread about R&S in the manga: The truth is, if someone who had never seen the show insisted you explain the entire premise, there's no way you could keep it from sounding EXTREMELY insane. EXPLAINER: "Well, a meteor crashed into the earth in 2000 in an event known as Second Impact, and now in 2015 these huge, strange creatures called Angels start attacking again (as apparently they were there in 2000), and robots called Evangelions were developed to fight them. One of the leads of NERV (the developers of the Evangelions), Gendo Ikari, called his son Shinji, whom he had previously disowned, to pilot one of the Evas. At first, he didn't want to pilot it, but then he saw how the previous pilot, a blue-haired girl named Rei Ayanami who looks bizarrely like his mother, had been terribly injured in combat and he didn't want to force that upon her again. He defeats the Angel and moves in with Misato Katsuragi, the woman who took him to NERV, who also has a genetically manipulated warm-water penguin who lives in her freezer and drinks beer. Then there's something about toothpicks, but I'm not quite sure what. So Shinji transfers schools, and this guy named Toji hates him because he squished his sister, but then they get to be friends during the next phallic Angel fight. Also, he makes a friend named Kensuke. Toji and Kensuke think Misato is hot. Then, Shinji accidentally walks in on Rei naked and accidentally gropes her. Next, Rei and Shinji fight a big octahedron Angel with a big laser. Rei doesn't know how to react, so Shinji tells her to smile and she smiles. Also, a red-haired Amerigerm girl named Asuka Langley Soryu moves in with Shinji and Misato and she yells at Shinji and stuff, but she really loves Kaji, a man who apparently works for NERV, but doesn't really, and who had a past relationship with Misato. Kaji also gave the first Angel (Adam, who was also the first human) to Gendo. Also, there's another Angel who's also Adam crucified on a huge red cross down in NERV's basement. I forgot to mention, by the way, that it wasn't really a meteor that caused Second Impact; it was actually an Angel. So Rei, Shinji and Asuka fight some more angels in the Evangelions, which aren't really robots but giant Angel clones with the souls of their dead mothers inside them, and then Shinji's Evangelion goes crazy, breaks free of her bindings, eats an Angel, assimilates Shinji, and then takes a nap. They get Shinji back, though. Kaji and Misato get back together, but Kaji gets shot by someone, I'm not sure who. That makes Asuka sad. She gets really depressed and runs away, while Rei dies in an Angel battle, but then she's okay, because they had several backup Reis. You know, because she's a physical copy of Shinji's mother. So a new pilot named Kaworu Nagisa arrives to replace Asuka, who is currently being held in a medicinally-induced coma so that she doesn't try anything funny. But it turns out he's an Angel and Shinji has to kill him. Kaworu also points out that the Adam on the cross isn't Adam at all, but Lilith. Rei has Lilith's soul, so she can fly now. Now Shinji gets all emo and suicidal and stuff. He tries to get Asuka to help him, but she's in a coma. I forget what happens next. So then Seele, who had been monitoring NERV and funding them and stuff, decides to kill everyone and start Third Impact. They get a bunch of Mass Produced Evangelions to eat Asuka and her Evangelion, then they turn Shinji's Evangelion into the Tree of Life. The Human Instrumentality Project starts, and everyone's mind merges together and Shinji has sex with everyone. Then Shinji decides to end Third Impact and the world is all gone and only he and Asuka are left. He decides to strangle Asuka, but then he stops." LISTENER: "Wow, that sounds like quite a show. What's it called?" EXPLAINER: " THE ARISTOCRATS! "

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jun 18, 2007 7:00 pm I would say, "Explain what?" Otherwise, you may as well write a book that follows every cut of every scene in order to clarify and explain everything (hey, kind of like the FGC).

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Jun 18, 2007 7:14 pm the brain . . it hurts . . .THE BRAIN!

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jun 18, 2007 7:18 pm LMAO & NAveryW! That was great! RE: Ornette - Yeah, I've basically said the same thing, but some people just don't understand how friggin MUCH of NGE there is to explain. I used the example that it's like asking someone to explain the Middle Earth Saga to you. It's pretty much impossible without a buttload of time and dedication. However, I'm trying to put something in print that coveres "the major" topics. The problem I've found with NGE is once you explain one thing, that one thing explained leaves 10 more questions. Q: What are the Evas? A: Beings created from Adam (and Lilith) with human souls in them. Q: How/why do they have human souls? Q: How were they built? Q: What are they for? Q: Why do they occasionally go nuts? Q: WTF happens to Sho in EoE? :shock: :evil:

[]:             Quote Post by Hailtheplatypus » Mon Jun 18, 2007 7:27 pm I would just look at them and say... you wouldnt believe me if I did... then curiosity would force them to watch the series. then PROFIT :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jun 18, 2007 8:04 pm Main explanation, IMHO: Eva's about people realizing its okay for them to live life and they CAN get over their problems as well as become happy provided they work on themselves first. The PRS, Classic "Mecha" story and the rest is all just window dressing. Shogoki no koto: Apparently her "lonely" jounry in space has to do with her finding a new planet to become the next SoL. Guess this means that anybody can become happy INCLUDING Yui-sama? ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Mon Jun 18, 2007 8:29 pm a lot of time and patience.

[]:             Quote Post by sensei_juice » Mon Jun 18, 2007 8:41 pm Eva is about all generalizations failing - it's about every different facet of life and how arbitrary life is, and how we can accept that randomness and just be ourselves or reject it and suppress ourselves or any combination of the two.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jun 18, 2007 8:46 pm sensei_juice wrote: Eva is about all generalizations failing - it's about every different facet of life and how arbitrary life is, and how we can accept that randomness and just be ourselves or reject it and suppress ourselves or any combination of the two.

[]:             Quote Post by Kyle » Mon Jun 18, 2007 9:51 pm I would just be like..."Dude......theres like...so many hot underaged girls." That would be it because I can't explane something I don't know how to explaine...it's really that simple,well at least with me.

[]:             Quote Post by Hailtheplatypus » Mon Jun 18, 2007 9:58 pm There isnt a way to explain Eva... If one did Eva justice in explaining it to someone who never saw it the world would implode and we would all turn to tang...

[]:             Quote Post by Kyle » Mon Jun 18, 2007 10:13 pm Hailtheplatypus wrote: There isnt a way to explain Eva... If one did Eva justice in explaining it to someone who never saw it the world would implode and we would all turn to tang...

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Tue Jun 19, 2007 3:58 am lol navery.. totally awesome

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Tue Jun 19, 2007 4:25 pm I wouldn't even try to explain Eva to another person. So much of the interpretation of it depends on your own point of view that you wouldn't be able to adequately explain it to another person who does not think like you. Basically the best I could do is tell them to watch the anime and figure it out themselves. NGE is not something you can be spoon feed.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 19, 2007 5:01 pm The glib explanation -- it's about the Human Condition, and whether to deal with it by escaping from it or embracing it. It's dressed up with sub-allegorical monster-of-the-week battles to give the characters something to really stress about.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Jun 19, 2007 6:20 pm I haven't heard anything so far that tops "mind fuck"

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Tue Jun 19, 2007 7:58 pm Person: Hey, can you explain Evangelion to me? You: 2 words... Mind Fuck.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jun 19, 2007 8:29 pm Opteron-O3 wrote: Person: Hey, can you explain Evangelion to me? You: *Slaps Person*

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Tue Jun 19, 2007 8:35 pm NAveryW wrote: Opteron-O3 wrote: Person: Hey, can you explain Evangelion to me? You: *Slaps Person*
